design_philosophy.core_loop: Build and manage an amusement park by constructing rides/shops, hiring staff, conducting research, setting prices, and balancing finances to increase park value and expand to new locations worldwide
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management under competing demands: maintaining profitability while expanding attractions, keeping staff and visitors satisfied, and preventing park deterioration through neglect
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a tycoon entrepreneur building a global amusement park empire from modest beginnings, with creative control over park design, customization, and strategic business decisions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Realism-focused simulation that treats business systems seriously (negotiations, strikes, logistics, stock management) rather than arcade mechanics; player agency in difficulty selection allows sandbox exploration or hardcore economic simulation; emergent problems (vandalism, breakdowns) create organic challenges rather than scripted obstacles

difficulty.checkpointing: Persistent park state; calendar-based yearly performance evaluation; ability to switch difficulty mid-game
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable difficulty levels with varying simulation depth: sandbox (no research/negotiations/stocks), medium (core management), and hard (full logistics including research allocation, wage negotiation, stock/share management). Player can adjust game speed and switch modes at any time. No fail state; losses represent poor strategy rather than game over.

progression.upgrade_system: Technology research system where player allocates funding to unlock new ride types, show acts, shops, facilities, and staff efficiency improvements. Rides can be made more durable through research. Staff capabilities scale with investment.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential research progression gates access to advanced attractions and features. Research funding allocation determines what becomes available, giving player strategic choice. Starting park has limited ride/shop palette; complexity expands through technological advancement.
replayability_hooks: Multiple worldwide park locations with distinct economies, weather patterns, terrain, and land values that create different strategic challenges
Customizable shop attributes (e.g., ice cream sugar content) affect visitor behavior and return rates
Trophy achievements for specific accomplishments (longest roller coaster, highest park value)
Three difficulty modes allow different playstyles
Emergent problem-solving: staff management quality, vandalism prevention, and ride maintenance create unique scenarios
Annual performance ranking against rivals creates competitive replayability

world_building_techniques: Geographic locations worldwide with distinct economic and environmental characteristics (desert, glacier, etc.)
Dynamic visitor behavior systems that respond to park conditions, pricing, and customization
Emergent narrative through staff interactions: negotiation scenarios, strikes, wage disputes
Social systems: visitor satisfaction/dissatisfaction leading to vandalism and crime as consequences
Seasonal and weather variations affecting park operations and visitor patterns

faction_indicators: Staff unions (represented through wage negotiation and strike mechanics)
Competing park operators (annual performance ranking creates implicit rivalry)
Visitor demographic preferences (behavioral customization based on shop/ride attributes)
danger_signals: Vandalism and crime emerge when visitor satisfaction drops due to poor management
Staff strikes and shipment loss represent economic leverage from organized labor
Ride explosions from neglected maintenance create cascade failures
Thugs explicitly attack park infrastructure (popping balloons, beating entertainers) as visible threat escalation
environmental_storytelling: Park cleanliness and maintenance state reflects management quality; messy footpaths signal neglect
Ride condition and capacity visible to players; deterioration is physical consequence not abstract stat
Visitor flow patterns and happiness indicators visible through park touring feature
Geographic setting variations (terrain, weather) create implicit narrative of business expansion challenges
Custom shop attributes create personalized park identity (themed ice cream flavors, etc.)
